intro: Ardisia zarceroana is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to Costa Rica, and is also known as Costa Rican boxwood or ardisia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ardisia zarceroana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ardisia zarceroana is often prepared as a tea or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, and it is also used in some commercial herbal supplements.
